summary: Hunter-Schreger bands are part of tooth structure derived during development; thus they originate from the same layer as dentin and cementum which form alongside them but do so independently without direct interaction or influence between each other beyond shared developmental timing within dental tissue formation processes. Other options like sweat glands clearly have different origins indicating non-ectodermic derivation making these incorrect choices compared against those correctly identified by this analysis focusing strictly upon etiology linked back towards embryonic layers giving rise only certain types of epithelial derivatives including teeth among many others listed here too narrowly scoped around just one
